name: Spangdahlem Air Base
tagline: Home of the 52nd Fighter Wing in Germany's scenic Eifel region, near Trier and the Luxembourg border.
_note: True OCONUS (Germany). OHA housing, DoDEA Europe schools, command-sponsorship/SOFA notes, no BAH/RentCast/SchoolDigger/rent-buy sections (US-only feeds). USAF 52nd Fighter Wing (F-16). Eifel region near Bitburg and Trier (Germany's oldest city); Luxembourg close. Medical: 52nd Medical Group clinic; complex care via Landstuhl. Facts verified from official sources (spangdahlem.af.mil, 52 MDG/TRICARE, DeCA/AAFES, DoDEA Europe, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx).

data.Near: Bitburg and Trier (Germany's oldest city); Luxembourg is close

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Spangdahlem Air Base is home to the U.S. Air Force's 52nd Fighter Wing, flying the F-16 Fighting Falcon and providing combat airpower across the European theater.

It sits in Germany's scenic Eifel region near the towns of Bitburg and Trier, the country's oldest city, with Luxembourg just to the west. The countryside setting gives families an authentic German experience with the rest of Europe close at hand.

sections.title: An Eifel Tour: On-Base vs. Living in a German Village

data.subtitle: A tour in the Eifel is a favorite. The main choice is on-base housing versus renting 'on the economy' in the surrounding German villages, each with real trade-offs.
intro: Most families come command-sponsored and choose between on-base housing and renting on the German economy with the Overseas Housing Allowance (OHA). The villages around Spangdahlem and Bitburg are popular with American families.

On base is the simplest, most American option with the shortest commute. Living on the economy means an authentic German-village experience, often with more space, balanced against a German lease, the language, and a commute through the countryside.

recommendations.advice: On-base housing keeps you close to work, schools, and services, ideal for a first overseas tour.
Renting in the surrounding Eifel villages and the Bitburg area offers the real German experience, with OHA to offset rent.
The housing office assigns on-base housing and must approve economy rentals, and helps with leases, utilities, and OHA, so start there before signing anything.
Trier, Luxembourg, and the Mosel wine region are close, and the rest of Europe is within easy reach for travel.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Access follows U.S. and host-nation (SOFA) rules. Visitors without a DoD ID need to be sponsored and registered through the Visitor Control Center.
Requirements for overseas installations can be stricter and change with force-protection levels, so confirm the current details on the official Spangdahlem site before you go.

sections.title: Housing (On-Base & OHA)
data: Spangdahlem has on-base family housing, and many families instead live on the German economy in the surrounding villages, receiving the Overseas Housing Allowance (OHA) to offset rent and utilities rather than the stateside BAH.
The housing office assigns on-base housing and must approve economy rentals; it also helps with leases, utilities, and OHA paperwork. Start there as soon as you have orders.

sections.title: Schools (DoDEA)
data: Children of U.S. personnel attend Department of Defense Education Activity (DoDEA) schools in the Spangdahlem and Bitburg community, which serve students from elementary through high school.
Confirm current schools, boundaries, and registration through DoDEA Europe and the base, and bring records and immunizations when you register.

data.Care: Outpatient primary and specialty care on base; inpatient and complex care via Landstuhl Regional Medical Center and host-nation hospitals

intro: An overseas PCS to Germany has extra steps beyond a stateside move. Get these sorted early to settle in smoothly.

steps.title: Confirm command sponsorship
steps.text: Make sure your family is command-sponsored before or upon arrival; it drives your OHA, DoDEA enrollment, and travel entitlements.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, update DEERS, and get overseas ID and logistics cards as required.

steps.title: Sort housing and OHA
steps.text: See the housing office first. Choose on-base housing or get approval and help to rent on the economy with OHA; claim Temporary Lodging Allowance (TLA) while you look.

steps.title: Handle SOFA, license, and vehicle
steps.text: Get your USAFE driver's license, register or ship your vehicle, and complete SOFA documentation. Confirm whether family members need a no-fee (official) passport.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Register with the DoDEA Spangdahlem/Bitburg schools early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Enroll in the TRICARE Overseas Program so you can be seen at the 52nd Medical Group clinic and host-nation providers.

data.q: Do I get BAH at Spangdahlem?
data.a: No. Overseas you receive the Overseas Housing Allowance (OHA) for economy rentals, or you live in on-base housing. OHA is based on your actual rent up to a cap, not the stateside BAH tables.
Either in on-base housing or on the German economy in the surrounding Eifel villages and the Bitburg area, using OHA.
Kids attend DoDEA schools in the Spangdahlem and Bitburg community, from elementary through high school.

data.q: Is it a good tour for travel?
data.a: Excellent. Trier, Luxembourg, and the Mosel wine region are close, and the rest of Europe is within easy reach by car or train.

picks.name: Trier
picks.blurb: Germany's oldest city, with Roman ruins like the Porta Nigra, nearby.

picks.name: Mosel Wine Region
picks.blurb: Steep riverside vineyards and wine villages along the Mosel.

picks.name: Luxembourg City
picks.blurb: The historic capital of Luxembourg, a short drive west.

picks.name: Eifel National Park
picks.blurb: Forests, lakes, and hiking across the volcanic Eifel region.
